questions.question: Which tribal leader led the Munda Ulgulan (rebellion) in the 1890s?
Aruna Asaf Ali is widely remembered for hoisting the Indian National flag during the Quit India Movement in 1942 at the ______.
Who was the sole representative of the Indian National Congress in the 2ⁿᵈ Round Table Conference of 1931?
Which of the following is INCORRECT with regard to the Swadeshi movement?
questions.option_2: Received wide support from upper class Muslims.
questions.option_3: Labourers carried out strikes at different parts of the country.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a reform suggested by Birsa Munda to the Munda tribal community during the Munda rebellion?
questions.option_1: Give up living in the past
questions.option_3: Give up believing in sorcery and witchcraft

questions.question: The export-led strategy of the British damaged the domestic economy. What was the impact of export-oriented policies on Indian food security?
Which of the following Acts abolished the dyarchy at provincial level during the colonial period in India?
What was the key electoral innovation introduced in the 1909 Act?
Which major reform was aimed at redistributing land to the landless after independence?
The office of Governor-General was renamed after 1858 as ____.
What was the main objective of the Pitt’s India Act of 1784, passed by the British Parliament?
Which movement marked Mahatma Gandhi’s entry into all-India politics?
Pazhassi Raja was a freedom fighter from the state of modern-day ___________.
Which policy was adopted by the British in the post-1857 revolt in India due to distrust of Hindu-Muslim unity?
Which foreign ruler invaded India in 1739, leading to the massacre of Delhi and further weakening the Mughal Empire?
Which of the following events led to the division of the Indian National Congress into Moderates and Extremists in 1907?

This freedom fighter did not share Gandhi’s ideal of ‘ahimsa’ and ‘satyagraha’. In 1941, he left his Calcutta home to Singapore via Germany and raised the Indian National Army. The leader in question is:
Which of the following major events followed the death of Potti Sreeramulu?
questions.option_1: Appointment of the States Reorganisation Commission

questions.question: The Sannyasi-Fakir Rebellion commenced in the second half of which of the following centuries?
To resolve peace following the riots that followed the Partition, the ‘Delhi Pact’ was signed between the Indian Prime Minister, Jawaharlal Nehru, and the Pakistan Prime Minister, __________.
What was the Extremists’ method to resist British policies apart from agitation and boycott?
